seoTitle: How to Create a Custom Form in Zendesk
metaDescription: Learn how to create a form in Zendesk, arrange custom fields, control end-user visibility, and scope forms to specific brands or subdomains.
intro: A single default ticket form can crowd customers with irrelevant fields and leave agents without the context they need. Zendesk's multi-form mapping engine lets you build targeted forms for specific issue types. This guide covers setting up a new form and controlling what fields appear.

steps.heading: Open the Forms manager
steps.body: In Admin Center, expand Objects and Rules and choose the Forms sidebar panel.

steps.heading: Create a new form
steps.body: Click Add Form and input an explicit administrative title for the new form.

steps.heading: Make it customer-facing if needed
steps.body: Check the Editable for End Users box to open the form to public customers.

steps.heading: Add fields to the layout
steps.body: Drag and drop custom field blocks from the right-hand palette into your main canvas layout.

steps.heading: Set field order and brand scope
steps.body: Rearrange field ordering and assign the form to specific domains or business lines if you have multiple brands.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Click Save to commit the form, then open a support draft or Help Center preview to confirm field ordering displays correctly.

faq.question: Can customers see every form I create?
faq.answer: Only forms marked as editable for end users appear on customer-facing intake pages.

faq.question: Can I limit a form to only certain brands?
faq.answer: Yes, forms can be scoped to specific brand subdomains rather than applying account-wide.

faq.question: What happens to existing tickets if I edit a form?
faq.answer: Form changes affect how new tickets are created without breaking legacy ticket histories.

faq.question: How do I reorder fields on a form?
faq.answer: Drag and drop the fields within the layout canvas to change their display order.
